i felt it was very very average for a Radisson Blu that happens to be in such a great location photos not indicative at all of a double room and the facilities listed were not available very disappointed as it was supposed to be a romantic night in town and the double room did not feel as luxurious as the information photos indicated there were no bathrobes no mini bar no iPod dock and the room felt very below average for the price paid  Concierge Staff were exceptional having stayed at a Radisson Blu before i love the way they greet their guests offer you a glass of water and show you to your room the room was at the back of the hotel so it was lovely and quiet having a bath in the bathroom was very nice

The room was very small with a bathroom door which only opened a third as the washbowl stopped it There was no bedside table on one side of of the bed as there was no room and where does one put a drink book and glasses We asked for a change after one night and although the room was still small it was a little better The single beds were uncomfortable and sloped so it was difficult to lie in a comfortable position and at aged 80 and 78 this was difficult We felt the hotel was tired and needed up grading We would not stay there again although the staff were very pleasant

Nothing to complain about   Staff were so accommodating on arrival our room wasn t quite ready but we only waited 5 10 minutes until one was available A bottle of wine in an ice bucket was delivered complimentary to our room as we were celebrating our anniversary We also asked for a late check out expecting a charge we were informed it was free

Bathroom dirty and poorly designed Towels hung over toilet nowhere to hang hand towel for sink Bath had hairs in on arrival waste pipe behind which could be clearly seen when showering very thick with dust Shower too close to wall and head not removable Like something out of the 80 s   Food in restaurant very good also good breakfast Location of hotel Room size
D cor seemed somewhat tired Pillows too high two were too much but one not enough   Loved the breakfast choice Nothing was too much trouble for the staff Comfy bed

The hotel has spent money on all the public areas but the bedroom we stayed in was looking very tired decor wise but the bathroom really needs upgrading the bath had been repaired at some point and the plastic coating was pealing off the grouting on the marble was very stained and the all the bathroom sealant on the taps soap holders etc was very messy and therefore makes the bathrooms harder to keep clean The toilet flush was not working properly The phone next to the bed was broken which was reported to reception So when I ordered room service at the bar the waiting staff were not familiar with the room menus As we were only staying for the night and leaving very early the next day these things were just annoyances but if I was staying for any length of time I would have asked for another room   The position on the hotel was excellent as we were visiting the Royal Albert Hall for a concert so it was easy to walk to The beds and pillows in the hotel room were excellent The person who delivered the room service was very pleasant
Room in front of the elevator quite noisy And as I had a single room it was quite tiny   Nice staff always ready to help and with genuine smile Great location Excellent breakfast

Too many doors to go through in order to get my room which was located at the farthest corner of the hotel especially when you re lugging heavy suitcases However I understand that this is a old building so it s not a big issue for me   Location just 5 minutes walk to the tube which is great Breakfast spread is moderate but for me is more than enough to fill me up However on a particular Saturday service staff were too slow in replenishing the empty trays leaving my guests standing by the buffet table waiting
Size of the room and brightness There was no view as it was at the back of the building and no natural light could get into the room   Excellent location and staff were fantastic

All in all it felt outdated especially the bathroom But it certainly has the English Charme I am more a scandivanian design person   Perfect Breakfast and conference Lunch Good location for Albert Hall and Kensington Gardens Close to Pubs and restaurants
arrived to check in told I think you at wrong hotel X but soon rectified but not in family room we booked and had to be separated on different floors  Bed comfy Breakfast que to long a wait

Arrived having prebooked Rooms 16 and 17 to be told that of those two only Room 17 would be available We were told that the hotel was overbooked Our friends therefore went into Room 29 There was also confusion about whether the rooms had both been charged to my card apparently one Room had been and the other had not been so one was charged again I will be checking my bank account this week to make sure that I haven t paid for more than two rooms Both rooms were cold problems with air conditioning units   Good location especially for the museums and the Royal Albert Hall Good evening meal and good breakfast Staff worked very hard and the service was good Beds very comfortable

The room was nothing like the image v small and cramped family accommodation Bed settee for the children was so uncomfortable they both slept on the floor When we went down for breakfast nobody to greet so wondered through the breakfast area and got shouted at by a member of staff to come out as not any room Lack of food prepared and had to wait for a considerable amount of time for food to be brought out the toaster was a domestic one which had seen better days and basically took an eternity to toast the bread so just gave up Poor quality and very over priced   Only good thing was the pleasant receptionist when we arrived

No fridge in my single room and no mirror near where I could plug in the hair dryer so made styling hair difficult Asked for room near my son and daughter in laws room but this didn t happen   This was a return visit this time with family Staff were lovely and helpful breakfast good but not as good as two years ago Location is excellent Would stay again and recommend to friends

Thought breakfast was a little expensive at 18 per head so opted to eat out   Proximity to tube station Hyde Park and museums Quiet room was good size and easy to find Reception staff were first class and maid service was very good

Rooms are really old carpet of the room very dirty The rooms of this hotel need some renovation Our first room was really poor with blinded windows After a complain we got another room and this was a bit better but the rooms really need an update Curtains also very dirty   Lobby of the hotel is nice and modern There is a nice bar and the location is perfect
Very small beds and room but comfortable  Great location perfect if you are visiting the Royal Alber Hall Natural History Museum The food in the restaurant was great

Halls are very narrow My extra large luggage had problems going through the doorways The stairs in the business suite were small and slippery with carpet on them   Great location 2 minute walk from the Piccadilly tube Great and friendly staff
Ordered room service and the price quoted on the info in the room was different to what we where charged in your favour  Staff very helpful on reception

Our room was right above a row of fans of some sort We could hear these even through the secondary double glazing   Friendly helpful staff and a fantastic breakfast with a vast choice of quality food changed frequently for freshness Ideal location for the Royal Albert Hall 10 minute walk as well as various museums a few minutes away
Biscuits in the room with the tea making facilities would have been a welcome touch   The location and the wonderful breakfast with the best sausages ever

The floor of my bathroom was badly stained and smelt especially around the toilet disgusting sums it up The housemaid occupied the one very slow lift and did not give way to an elderly guest Unable to open the window and the room was hot   Check in staff were friendly

The bedroom must have been in close proximity to the boiler plant room as every time the boilers pumps started the vibration and noise was felt through the bed Yes a vibrating bed not good So whilst the bed was comfortable the nights sleep was not   The bed the breakfast the staff
Room a bit chilly and draft by window Turned on heating got too hot and a bit noisy   Staff were extremely helpful and friendly Made very welcome from moment entered foyer

Room service took AGES Despite ringing down twice However when the club sandwich finally arrived it was delicious my complaint was sorted out very efficiently see above   Great room for a single the bed was comfy the room quiet A great location 2 mins walk from the tube station and less than 10 minutes from the Natural History museum and the V A Harrods 20 min walk and Hyde Park 10 mins The bar area was nice if minimal for evening drinks not too busy and the breakfast was really good with lots of choice We had the full English buffet followed by mini pain au chocolate and all was lovely I d definitely stay here and would recommend it BTW when I complained about the long delay with room service I was dealt with by Danielle who was very apologetic and sorted things out efficiently and courteously thank you
Bacon was a little cold from being in the servers dishes too long   pillows were fantastic staff very helpfull and a nice sitting area to meet friends

Being taken to a back lift and having to walk a very long way to my room when there was a lift close to it Odd  The staff were excellent Very friendly and helpful Loved the restaurant Had the best shepherds pie

Lack of gluten free bread was an inconvenience as we had been told it would be available Barman did not know what sherry was and offered me port until I persuaded him to look more carefully at which he found the Bristol Cream   Very comfortable bed excellent cooked breakfast

The first night we got a so called update of our room but it was a room without heating ice cold no view at all We called the housekeeper at 11 p m The whole part of the hotel in which was our room was non heated in november in London When we asked them to check the heating the only proposal was to give us a portable heating for the night So we asked to change the room next morning and then we got finally a nice room   Great location nice neighbourhood very comfortable

Only 1 lift very very slow mostly used by housekeeping If you have heavy luggage you might need extra 15 minutes for checkout just because of the lift Very low quality baby accessories cot high chair in restaurant In terms of baby friendliness they are still in the 90 s   Friendly staff nice bar decor Convenient location if you re into museums

Bedroom 428 was a little tired decor wise carpet grime looking bed too firm pillows too big sink partially blocked Breakfast much too expensive   Friendly helpful staff nice reception bar and restaurant areas Great location

The room was a little compact and as it was on the ground floor there was no opportunity for getting any fresh air My partner is quite tall and the bed was small for him also the bathroom was a little cramped and you had to sit on the toilet sideways to fit your legs in   Breakfast was really good value for money and the hostess booking us in was such a warm person as were the reception staff The location was fabulous for our stay close to the Royal Albert Hall museums South Kensington etc and such easy access to the tube to get anywhere else you wanted to go

Very small room could almost not walk around the bed no furniture beside the bed to put some night things Very small bathroom no space to put for 2 person toilletries had to use the toilet cover to put things down   Great location
Value was good for Kensington but the single room was very small   The staff were unfailingly courteous The buffet breakfast was excellent

Water collected in the bath while I was showering and the plug hole looked a little rusted I wasn t quite sure how to empty it There were a great many people to cater for in the restaurant in the evening Friday night so this meant the staff were under pressure and therefore a little slow   Very good choice at breakfast Staff were very helpful upgraded my room at no extra cost Very comfortable large bed Couldn t hear anyone else near me which was good Room with a fridge an extra bonus Ease of checkout could have checked in or out online Couldn t see the prices on the a la carte menu but the food was excellent I loved the whitebait
Bit noisy as our room was on road Double room was small  Paid to Upgrade to a better room as our initial room was small and uninviting Staff pleasant and good location

Fabulous room as always Massive bed Soo comfortable Always feels very special staying here Staff are VERY smart and courteous Food is just wonderful Every meal I ve had here is better than I imagined it would be A lovely hotel in a great location

One room was a tiny single but OK The double room was basically a single room with a double bed squeezed in at double the cost and this ploy was made all the more blatant by a small table only one chair and only one bedside table and lamp and one had to climb over the bed to get to the other side This is why I gave the lowest score for value for money Also the room to room phone service did not work and was not fixed despite alerting the staff and two room cards failed and had to be replaced The TV is rather complicated and it might be easy accidentally to incur extra charges   good location friendly staff decent if expensive breakfast

Hairs of all kinds in the bath was a bit of a shock   The staff was wonderful throughout the hotel The feel and scent when you arrived in the lobby was beautiful The bed and pillows were the best I ve ever had in a London hotel
Couldn t get a cup of tea after 11 30 pm but not a real problem otherwise no complaints at all   Bed was very comfortable and the breakfast really very good Staff were charming

The tiny bathroom with low down washbasin and difficult to access bath ought to have been made into a shower room The easy chairs were completely worn out needed re upholstering The lack of space generally for a luxury room was very disappointing   The bed was clean and comfy and the hotel was situated where we wanted to be
The room was very dated and tired It looked nothing like the ones shown on the website The shower was either scalding or freezing Not the standard I expect at a Raddisson Blu  The checkout app

When I booked I requested gluten free food and it was in capital letters on the booking form When booking in at reception I enquired about the gluten free bread at breakfast and was told that there would be some The first morning was fine but on Sunday we went to breakfast at 9 30 ordered gluten free toast the waiter said after 10 mins when I enquired that it was on its way but after 20 mins he came back to say there wasn t any but did I want any other sort of bread I AM A COELIAC AND CANNOT EAT ANY OTHER SORT OF BREAD Breakfast finishes at 11am so the catering for gluten free was very poor and after all I had requested at the time of booking   We did get discounted for the lack of gluten free but I feel I should point it out as I am very ill if I eat gluten The reception people were exceptional with taxis etc

Prices in the bar are very expensive   The reception staff were excellent phoned us to say room was ready left our bags earlier in the day these were in our room ready for us staff suggested places to go Room was comfy

Drinks and coffee at bar were very expensive and we were charged 98p service charge to have coffee at the bar   Location was perfect Staff were very helpful Breakfast was good Room was small but well equipped

Nothing all good   I traveled shortly after surgery to my hand and was somewhat apprehensive as to how I would manage with my arm in a sling I needn t have worried all of the staff were very helpful and friendly making my stay very comfortable and enjoyable
Some kind of drawers or shelves for clothing would be very helpful Water pressure in the shower could be stronger   Staff were very friendly and helpful The location is wonderful
Breakfast on last day monday was poor compared to previous days  Staff very friendly and helpful Room spacious superior Great for tube and museums
Location is good not too far from Heathrow Airport and a few minutes walk to exibitions and Hyde Park Staffs are helpful We left early on the departture day and we got breakfast box
An issue with one of the rooms on arrival was sorted out quickly and to our satisfaction   Ideal location for our needs good breakfast buffet clean and comfortable room

Needed to use machine to get wheelchair up the steps didn t realise there was no street entry ramp or ordinary access This took time and made us feel rather trapped Bathroom completely unsuitable and Ill equipped Impossible to use shower needs an accessible fixed seat Request for a chair produced no result or answer Did ask on booking form for shower which could be used with a wheelchair and no indication of building access difficulty  Pierre very helpful moving the wheelchair onto the machine to go up the steps into the hotel All staff willing and helpful
I cannot think of anything that could have been done better   I was particularly impressed by the courtesy of the staff and the comfort of the room and hotel generally

Bathroom is a touch small for such a large room but it s an older building of course it s London and still totally functional It would be nice if the door did not have glass and was solid as it lights the room in the morning Just a personal opinion of course Not something to deter staying here   Staff especially nice and helpful just what you would expect Style decor and location are excellent Breakfast was really delicious buffet and service and the restaurant staff was very helpful Room was very large and quiet even though in the front of the hotel Really a great experience Would recommend highly

The building is nice and in a good location for our trip but the experience as a whole was very disappointing For the amount of money we paid we definitely expected at better level of service and facilities Breakfast and dinner were very poor we ve paid the same amount of money else where and got better service and a bigger room A premier inn would have been better value for money this time round

Standard room was rather small and if you sat on the toilet in the cramped bathroom your knees touched the sink  Excellent breakfast waiting staff friendly and helpful despite it being very busy Good location close to tube and museums

Great location BUT my single room s width was exactly the same length as a single bed No room to swing a cat It was like sleeping in a cupboard Although the room was clean the furnishings were very tired Breakfast food not restocked well enough So at 200 night this all seemed pretty poor value   Friendly staff Quality yoghurts at breakfast
room service very bad and lazy corridor very narrow  close to Gloster tube station good rich breakfast with fruit and vegetable stuff very helpful

The outlook The mirrors were too much for our taste   Pretty quiet with a comfortable sitting space in the room We turned up early prepared to drop our bags and check in later but the room was ready ahead of the scheduled check in time
The room was small given the price and the bathroom was not particularly user friendly Overall the hotel felt a bit tired   The bed was comfortable and the breakfast was good value
The room size was a bit small for price   Short walk from Gloucester Rd station One of greatest breakfast I ve had in the uk Friendly staff The room was comfortable and clean

Tiny elevator ill informed staff and price was much too high I was traveling with a business partner that wasn t an option above The building is very tiny and cramped so I don t feel the lofty price was warranted Very deceptive online photos that appeared to be expansive rooms with old world grandeur Also a very scary man stalked me while I was there He saw me in the bar and followed me for the entire evening I was terrified I stay in Radissons in US   Bed was wonderful

The hallways and bathrooms are tight and small making them hard to mauvauer For a London Hotel you expect to pay high prices but you also expect a certain level of professionalism That was lacking Overall the hotel was comfortable but totally forgettable For the same price I d choose a different hotel  Breakfast provided variety in a buffet style and the room bathroom and bed provide the comfort you d expect from the price and loction
Bathroom tiny not much room to manoeuvre   Prime location within easy walk of several family attractions Breakfast and restaurant food good quality

The bedroom was small and dark The bathroom could not have been smaller We were put in a twin room having requested a double We were advised to book with the hotel directly next time rather than online The welcome note on the desk in the room was addressed to a different client Not a good experience for a weekend break   The location was alright

There was a short circuit in the room when my wife plugged in her hair dryer We had never experienced such poor facility in any other hotel before Rooms are extremely small and walls were stained Lift was too small Would accommodate 3 people at max I would not recommend this to anyone   Location was convenient and close to Royal Albert hall
The only thing I could fault was the cold plates Cold plates with hot food do no mix It s a small complaint and easily rectified   Very clean and tidy Lots of friendly helpful staff

Our room was on the road and Cromwell road is very busy the traffic is constant so the noise was a factor when trying to sleep our stay was only three nights don t think we had more than a couple of hours sleep the first two The shower was running hot and cold not such a big deal but could be annoying slow service in the restaurant   The bed was comfortable staff were so friendly and helpful could not fault them great breakfast to set you up for the day and the location was great we could walk to most of the attractions we wanted to visit the tube station was a short walk away for everything else I liked the don t clean my room today feature meant housekeeping weren t knocking on the door and money was being donated to charity great idea

Comfortable family room for 2 adults and 2 children in central and family friendly London location close to Hyde Park Princess Diana Memorial Playground Natural history museum and Picadilly tube line to Covent Garden for shows Gloucester Rd tube stop just across the street
Room is too small bathroom tiny it was hard to get to the tank it is very small Bathroom equipment is very old The hotel is like a labyrinth with narrow halls  Breakfast and location

We were booked for twin beds got a double Very small room with dirty old carpets and an extremely small bathroom with stained toilet Breakfast was chaotic not enough tables a long cue and the cooked food was not hot The staff were nice and doing their best BUT Not an impressive place to stay Upstairs it needs to be completely refurbished I would rate the lifts and passageways upstairs as a bad hazard in case of emergency Overall I would try and find somewhere else to visit  The staff were kind and helpful

As someone from the US I would have liked colder AC but keep in mind we were there on the hottest days in Sept in 111years   The restaurant was lovely we had the included breakfast a lunch and a dinner there all were top notch Afternoon sweets in the lobby were a pleasant surprise touch Staff was very kind

Narrow long dark hallways can make you feel claustrophic if you are prone to that and the rooms are incredibly small albeit very nice and the bathroom was modern and nice so all you really need   Great hotel clean comfortable delicious breakfast in a wonderful open airy room Close walk to tube
Initially the TV was out of order but it was repaired by the time we returned from the theatre   The breakfast was excellent and the staff most courteous

Room smallish and no storage space We were upgrade to a bios was class room which was pleasant but total lack of storage for more than one pair of underpants and a couple of shirts in the wardrobe  The location is very conve
Rooms are small   Location takes 1 minute to the underground Near Hyde Park staff are helpful the hotel is surrounded by many resturants and coffee shops

At this price I expected a a good sized room it was small and pokey b attention to detail some parts of the room needed repainting repair   Breakfast was excellent The Scoff Banter was a pleasant environment in which to eat Manageress was helpful

Vent in bedroom dripped water and the bathtub was a death trap no bathmat and difficult to get into nearly fell twice getting in and out Hairs found on the bedsheets When I came back to my room one evening at 9pm the housekeeper was standing in the middle of the room looking surprised That was truly odd Staff held my luggage the day I checked out and when I returned to retrieve it they couldn t find it and asked me where it was How on earth should I know You re the ones who took it Room was near the lift and was EXTREMELY loud at all hours of day and night with drunk guests coming in and shouting down the corridor and no sound proofing it would seem Great location and decor in the corridors stay let down by oddly behaved staff wayward hairs and a dripping vent Wouldn t return and FWIW this is the last time I ll choose a Radisson Blu They always seem to be a bit odd   Location near the Underground was nice and great walking distance to Kensington museums etc Free bottled water in the room

I asked for extension of stay and they confirmed it but next day they called me when I was in University and asked me to leave the room immediately as their colleague made a mistake in extending my room and rooms are now occupied I had to cut my class and come back to hotel in the afternoon to check out

Twin room far too small There was no where to put luggage or a tray to put a room service order Tv very poor quality Bathroom so small it was dangerous getting out of the bath shower The most disappointing room that we have ever stayed in So bad that one person had to stay in bed whilst the others person manovered around the room
While the bed was very comfy the comforter was way too hot for the summer weather  The staff was amazing Very friendly and very willing to meet any need we had
Staff recognized me and were always helpful and interactive Good breakfast selection and fresh food This was my Second Stay and I felt relaxed looked after and special

See above comments  Good location and room had tea and coffee Room was small and had no bedside tables Hairdryer was in a drawer with the wire connected so very difficult to use as it only stretched a short way Bathroom was clean and had good facilities but both our room and my daughters had a big black ring in the toilets so that let the room down a bit Nice big fluffy towels Only one small lift which only fitted 3 people and very dark corridors it just seemed a maze of corridors and stairs Probably wouldn t stay there again

Room was slightly crowded due to small size however can t complain as it was a lovely stay the room was very lovely overall   Very friendly member of staff who greeted me and my boyfriend upon arrival unfortunately I didn t catch his name Lovely hotel which smelled so fresh as soon as you walked in the door Very nice room also with air con which was a great bonus as the weather was so hot Short walk to Gloucester Road underground station and situated in a stunning area Will definitely be back to stay at this hotel and see what more it has to offer

There was no spa facility such as a sauna or steam room which isn t the biggest of issues but would be a nice addition to the gym they have   The overall stay was enjoyable staff were friendly The location is superb

Wifi variable Bedroom and bathroom very small no room for bedside tables or drawers air conditioning barely adequate poor for a 4 star hotel   Good location Comfortable bed and quiet night Staff tried hard

Nothing cannot fault the hotel   The service was warm and friendly and nothing was too much trouble for them The room was clean and one of the most comfortable beds that I have ever slept in The decor was very tasteful and yet contemporary
there was not any room to walk around to the closet I would recommend a queen size bed and smaller chairs We were in room 226   Close to museums subway helpful and friendly staff

The pillows were no good for me so soft they swallowed my head I never realised you could ask for different pillows though A view would have been nice too but the room facing back meant there was no street noise   There s a reason a lot of reviews mention the breakfast It was fantastic The staff were very friendly and helpful which was important for a weary traveler The building was awesome it had an old building feel while still keeping up with modernity
Nothing   Excellent location comfortable bed nicely decorated clean rooms I had read reviews that the standard single rooms were too small however I found mine was more than enough for what I needed

After seeing lovely pictures on the web I was a little disappointed with the room Although perfectly well equipped clean and comfy it was no more glamorous than a Premier Inn   Beds were comfy Room was clean On request the staff dealt with an issue that we had with noisy neighbours
Rooms were a little small   Great location within 5 min walk of Natural History museum and V A Very friendly helpful staff Great choice of breakfast in lovely bright airy room
the location was the best next to the little treats in my room for my birthday it made my weekend Also the sweet treats in the lobby Such a lovely touch Keep it up
Nothing we enjoyed a lovely short stay   Great London hotel excellent staff and good facilities Close to trains buses and in walking distance to Harrods and other retail

The TV system was a little sluggish moving between channels and sometimes the AC system left the room with an odd smell but it wasn t really noticeable and turning off the AC for a time sorted the problem   The breakfast is excellent and the staff are very helpful The location is also excellent There is a room with 3 internet connected macs connected to a printer so you can print off your boarding pass I would stay here again

Carrying luggage up 25 stairs from the tube to the street was a real challenge yet we were willing to strain ourselves for the proximity of the station The only other negative is we couldn t stay longer than our 3 night stay   What a beautiful jewel of a boutique hotel From the moment we arrived we knew we were staying in a very special place The staff was attentive helpful and made sure we were happy with our room which was bright spacious and a most comfortable king sized bed The rooms are classic and understated with soothing colors The linens towels and soap were of the highest quality I could imagine the Vanderbilts gathering around the marble fireplace in the waiting area adjacent to the front desks The charming fresco ceiling enhanced the feeling of a bygone era We were able to take the Tube directly from Heathrow to Gloucestor Station which was less than a block from the hotel and very convenient as we used the Tube often Very centrally located we were able to easily walk to Royal Albert Hall the Victoria and Albert Museum and the hop on hop off buses Breakfast is served in a lovely dining room and was generously appointed with many options from a simple continental breakfast to full English breakfast with everything in between like fresh juicy strawberries to several types of yogurt Truly this hotel exceeded our expectations We felt very lucky to have discovered it
Rooms would have been too small if staying lomger Staff forgot to replace tea cups and water glasses   Very stylish and boutique Restaurant food and bar good Staff helpful
Wonderfully quiet Hotel with comfort put first and foremost would reccomend to all to stay here We will be returning again hopefully not too distant future

Although very clean I feel that the sheer curtains in our room are due to a dry clean Room 428   The staff were very helpful and attentive I would commend the Duty Manager who was on duty Thursday 11 August I m sorry but can t remember her name She was extremely helpful with some property we had left behind and I am very grateful

Rooms are compact but comfortable It is a maze to get to your room and lifts very small and not very useable  Easy access for location to city and other areas Check in was not easy as error on room but fixed quickly Staff friendly and left you alone or helped as required which was nice as they were in background and not in your face

A couple of little things 1 We were in out of our room which seemed to cause some confusion around getting our room serviced if staff were requested to come back a little later 2 It wasn t until the last day when it was explained the cups glasses taken from our room weren t replaced until after 4pm Both could be resolved by better staff communication   Excellent welcome and support from the front desk including in booking shows etc Very good buffet breakfast and a reasonable range of wines and other drinks in the bar Outstanding location near the Gloucester Road tube restaurants and pubs and museums parks Our room was a good size comfortable although the shower in the bath in a tight ish bathroom might be a little difficult for some personally I d replace the baths with just showers A little quirky adventure navigating to our room the hotel grew from joining a number of prior apartment buildings but great character

The rooms are very small especially for families Not A family friendly hotel rooms were not deeply Cleaned full of dust Not enough storage in rooms   Location of the hotel People working at the reception

We arrived at the hotel at 11pm and we were greeted by a friendly receptionist who gave us the key to our room Upon entering the room I spotted T shirts on the bed and thought how lovely to receive complimentary nightwear Looking round the room I then spot slippers and a toilet bag and realized to my astonishment that we were in someone else s room Needless to say we vacated that room very quickly Luckily no one was in the room We proceeded to go back to reception and explained the situation He was very apologetic and gave us the key to our room The room we were given was a single superior but we were so tired by now that we couldn t face going back down again I had actually booked a double room My husband had an uncomfortable night I m sure had we had the right size bed it would have been fine   I would stay there again but next time I would make sure I get the right room

A C didn t really cool the room and the window didn t open room was hot and stuffy  Staff really friendly and helpful and great location close to Gloucester Road tube station Comfortable although small room

While the room was clean nonsmoking it was actually quite smoky needed to be aired out Otherwise a very clean room  Breakfast service was excellent staff was very attentive friendly Room was comfortable well equipped

The lift elevator is tiny and can barely take more than 3 people The stairs are badly signposted which doesn t help Heard people talking in the next room early in the morning though they may just have been loud   I stayed in this hotel previously in 2009 my single room was so small it felt like a wardrobe So this time I booked a superior single Worth the extra Room was a good size and nicely furnished It was facing not the road but wasn t at all noisy Staff were incredible helpful I didn t dine there so can t offer an opinion on that
Continental breakfast not the best Had room service coffee cold Reception seating area a little small   Restaurant excellent Nice atmosphere Good mixture of old and new
they have different room size some are very small and this not shown when you booking  the staff specially Mr Stefano Paganelli like to thank him for his help

Small bathroom but it didn t really matter   Best hotel bed ever Great pillows Dark when curtains shut for a really good sleep Great decor Lovely bar where you can actually get a drink that is not prosecco

The only downside was my bed the room I was in NEEDS a new bed it was so wore that you got in and rolled to the edge it was only a single room bed so didn t really have anywhere to move to This was very uncomfortable and didn t sleep too well   Location is brilliant just over the road from Gloucester Road Tube station Staff are very helpful especially the restaurant staff I was travelling alone and had breakfast by myself each morning there was a lady working there Celia I think her name was she made me feel so welcome and always come over for a chat Breakfast was amazing would go back just for that eggs benedict each morning as well as Danish pastries the choices were endless Room was clean big enough for me bathroom was spotless

Very warm welcome from a nice Indian man Got an upgrade for business At check out Daniela was very nice It was worth it First time in Edwardian hotel but not the last

Way too expensive for the sizes of the rooms Rooms were tiny in the 1 person room the door could not even open fully because it hit the bed already way too small Does not meet the Radisson Blu quality hotel facilities also outdated stuck in the early 2000s does not even have the universal sockets in the rooms Definitely should not be using the Radisson Blu name   Location was very good tube station close by with 3 lines

no small table in room nosurfaces shelves in bathroom to put down washbags Room service sandwich was enormous and came with masses of chips Too much to be called a sandwich   very pleasant staff food very good inrestaurant

Our Deluxe room looked like that of a premier inn when we complained that the amount of money we were spending on staying in that room was not proportionate we were told that was their best deluxe room We were moved to one of their newly renovated rooms and it was far superior to what we were given initially For a start the room was twice the size and so was the bathroom Our new room also had a settee in it Another thing we were not too pleased about was that whilst waiting for our new room to be ready we had to wait over an hour we decided to have a drink in the bar 2 pints of Peroni came to 14 as it included a service charge For them to pass the glasses over the bar To us And an hours wait for my sandwich from room service when I was told it would be 20 minutes   our bags were held for us until our room was ready Problems were sorted out quickly Very close to Gloucester Road tube station Room was beautiful

When we left we asked the front desk in the lobby to order a taxi for us When I order a taxi in London I expect it to be a London Cab or at least a cab from a recognized regulated and above board Company This didn t happen When it turned up it was a private car not marked as a taxi no meter or anything that normally comes with a taxi And at least the staff in the lobby should have asked me if it was OK to order a non taxi I could of course have rejected it but was a bit pushed for time and therefore the weak part in this When we arrived at Victoria the driver asked an exorbitant 35 When we arrived in London on Monday the same trip from Victoria to Cromwell Road cost 12 And taxi trip calculators for London gives the Price for that trip at 11 12 poungs So this non taxi driver charged three time the going rate There is no other way to describe this as a totally unacceptable rip off Even though the hotel is well located the staff is friendly etc the incident described above Means that I will never return to that hotel or any other hotel in the same chain I hope you will post this on your website so that other customers are warned off this hotel   The staff was very friendly and helpful with as it turned out one important exception

nothing  The staff were so helpful friendly the breakfast was wonderful the beds oh so comfy we loved it would most definitely return

Full_Review: I felt and hurt and cut my self badly in the bathtub their tubs are poorly designed they have a incline section at both ends and their shower heads are placed at a point where it forces you to step in the inclined area of the tub and as anyone can imagine if you are showering normally you will be using soap and this makes this inclined area very slippery The air conditioning was not working well and when we asked they sent some one to check but the first question was where are you from which basically meant what do you expect this is the Mercure hotel The evening manager did not seemed to enjoy his job always with an attitude   Most of the staff were friendly and attentive

Full_Review: Wifi code was daily so you would need to be clear about how many days you need access for   Nice room nice bathroom tea coffee in the room I asked for a quiet room and got one Breakfast was more than enough for anyone and the hotel was directly opposite the exhibition centre I was visiting No restaurant but plenty of places close by to eat at and opposite the tram stop and metro plus they gave me a voucher for a free drink at the bar what more can I say a good experience Also used the transfer service recommended by Booking com so had a car arranged in advance to drive from CDG to hotel and return two days late Thanks for the recommendation

Hotel_Name: Mercure Vaugirard Paris Porte De Versailles
Full_Review: I did not feel welcome at all 1 My checkin time was around 7pm and somehow there was only 1 assistant in the reception The other guy left and did not show up any more The only one person tried her best but for me it looked like a bad organisation of duties 2 My room key did not work They sent me back to the room 2 times after rebooting the key It turned out that the door lock did not work So finally I was given another room But before that I needed to go back and forth with my luggage 2 times trusting their words it should work They offered some drinks as a compensation but the drinks never appeared 3 The hotel looked like needing refurbishment dark and worn off The room was still quite ok 4 The breakfast wasn t expensive however rather limited in choice The service was non existent Personnel deliberately speaking French even if they understood that that s not my language   Bed was very good bath ok

Hotel_Name: Mercure Vaugirard Paris Porte De Versailles
Full_Review: The staff was a bit rude Maybe because of a language barrier We asked the staff to see if they do currency exchange because on their electronic board it showed the currency value and we thought Oh that s better than some of the places we saw on the streets and one of the staff said they don t do it in this hotel so she told us to go to Montparnasse Bienvenue We asked for the name of the Currency exchange place but she just told us to go there We got there and we got lost and confused We went to the other Mercure s that was in that area but they all had language barriers It was just a waste of time We ended up going to some Change place somewhere else We also had a Do Not Disturb Sign on our door because we don t feel comfortable people coming into our hotel room with our stuff out in the past we had hotel housekeeper steal things from us so it was just a mindset thing The housekeeper still came into our room and cleaned it up I mean what s the point of having that sign Sure we might be a little picky But it s better safe than sorry right What if that happened to you If we needed something we would call down to receptions   I liked the size of the room it was pretty big The bed was comfortable The rooms were clean The location was decent Just a 40min metro ride to places into Paris and the metro is literally right outside so it made location fair Should get a visitor pass to make it easier for the trip so you don t literally have to keep paying for the metro every time you take it

Hotel_Name: Mercure Vaugirard Paris Porte De Versailles
Full_Review: The hotel will lock the front main door at night and you have to phone the receptionist in order to open the door I had to wait several minutes until the receptionist opened the door because he was the ONLY person who were on duty that night it would be better if there are two or three persons stand by I would say   The fact that the tram and metro station is only 20 second walk even less Thts awesome

Full_Review: The hotel has two floors with rooms where smoking is allowed Unfortunately we got such a room Not very happy because I suffer from asthma There was no option to change to another non smoking room Did not know smoking was still allowed in European hotels Next time I book a hotel I will specifically request a non smoking room just to be sure   Location is great Rooms are quiet and clean

This was labeled a 4 Star hotel yet no A C only two outlets one of which was by the door so not useful no coffee maker no iron barely any walking room We had a triple room for myself my husband and 16 year old daughter we each got a tiny twin size bed one of which was a spring mattress cot Bathtub glass door rubber bottom broken so water leaked all over the floor Truly one of the worst hotels I have stayed Stayed at another 4 Star hotel in London few days prior with a complete opposite experience so I am comparing hotels within the same city   Nothing
Hotels in London in general are overpriced  Hotel is very well located some restaurants are nearby which is convenient when you are arriving at midnight

I chose this hotel for a special weekend away with my wife I had stayed at other Grange hotels on business and based on this positive experience I opted for the Grange Rochester I disliked my trip for three reasons We were staying in a poky top floor room with sloping ceilings I am 6 4 and it was claustrophobic to put it mildly We didn t wish to spend any more time than absolutely necessary in that room While the room was reasonably appointed it was shabby in parts and in need of a refurb as well as being cramped My second disappointment was during check in when the receptionist beamed at two other guests and told them the good news that they d been upgraded to a better room This only exacerbated my disappointment at our room especially on a special trip I appreciate that not everybody can have an upgrade but there must be a more discrete way of telling the lucky guests who do perhaps take them to one side etc Thirdly we arrived before our room was available for check in It was a very hot day and we opted to wait in the air conditioned bar and have a coffee It was impossible to find anybody to serve us and I had to go to reception three times before a waiter finally appeared
There was no air conditioning During a heat wave in the summertime proved to be a tad uncomfortable   Comfy beds great bathroom nice large windows loved the lobby bar elevator everyone was nice

No air conditioning was very hot so could not sleep all night opened windows but noisy road Also no where to get snacks or cold drinks my grandson and I had to walk outside and find somewhere to get snacks and cold drinks   location was good within walking distance of Westminster and Victoria station

Old Old Old Room needs a refurb The bed was low on slats and was one of those flip up to the wall type Creaked and felt like it could collapse at any time especially with duct tape all of the legs The shower was poor and a huge step up into the tub   Location was good and room looked out on to a lovely green square

No air conditioning on the hottest weekend of the year but appreciate the weather in the UK is rarely like that Disappointed at the fact that the wine bar closed really early when we would have eaten there every night because the food was so good We ve stayed here two years on the trot and would have stayed again the same time next year however I m disappointed that the price seems to have trebled for the same time next year so we won t be returning   Value for money location fabulous the food in the wine bar attached was fantastic

The cost of breakfast was extortionate even for London so we ended up at a coffee shop down the road I booked a twin executive room via booking com which advertised a balcony standard twin had same facilities except for a trouser press balcony Upon arriving at the hotel we entered our room to find we had been given a standard room and all executive twin rooms were fully booked the staff stated that executive was based on the size of the room not whether a balcony was included hence why I believe this is false advertising on booking com Staff ended up putting myself friend in a double room with balcony which we were happy with   Quiet location Nice view of the green Staff were very friendly After being provided with the incorrect room the staff we re accommodating resolved the issue

We were booked into a room with 2 double beds The room was small so our suitcases had to go on one of the beds The bed we slept on was very small for 2 people plus it was very hot Breakfast wasn t available until 8am and as we needed to leave by 8 to catch a bus we didn t have breakfast Also check in isn t until 3pm Very expensive   Handy location
Loved the location and selection of restaurants abs shops close by Also easily accessible either on foot or local transport to all other tourist atractions
Breakfast if added on was expensive  excellent value for money given its in Westminster The rooms overlooking the park were so quiet Quietest hotel I have ever had in London Clean and comfortable
Food was expensive but that might just be London   The location was very convenient to seeing the top attractions in London Self con tained with food and bar

I couldn t reach the bath shower controls without stepping into the bath and as this might have meant my head getting soaked I didn t use the bath shower at all the glass panel was in the way of the controls badly designed   bed was comforable

Great classy hotel with pleasant warm welcoming staff Lovely location not far from the centre of london leading to the landmarks I was down in london dj ing at an event and the hotel fit perfectly to relax beforehand and after i returned Would highly recommend and definately considering coming again for future preference if im back in the london area

The only thing that let our room down was the view which looked out onto another building over a busy road although that didn t affect our stay as we were out most of the time We slept with the windows open as the weather was really warm and the traffic was noisy There were 2 electric fans provided in the room which we did use during the little time we spent in the room during the day   The hotel was in an ideal location only 10 minutes walk from the high street shops restaurants and both rail and tube stations at Victoria All of the hotel staff were helpful and nothing was a problem We asked to borrow an iron ironing board when we checked in and it was delivered as soon as we reached our room The hotel is full of old world charm and we would definitely stay here again
The only milk provided was whole milk and the breakfast tea provided and served was unpalatable   Close proximity to local sites Homely and comfortable

Just a very good experience Warm welcome with polite attentive staff With comfortable stylish rooms and a lovely ambience throughout That s it

Lounge bar area not within hotel Breakfast not included Room too small En suite very small and dated Room too dark with no view Price rather high at 137 50 for back room and no breakfast   Very comfortable beds Decor very dated Room small and rather dark Staff very friendly and helpful

I had asked for a room with a shower with a hose The first room they showed me did not have this facility and the room was very narrow and faced on to the main road with passing traffic noise It could have done with a lot of changes When I complained they put in me a basement room with a double bed suitable wardrobe and other facilities The shower over the bath was on a hose but the basin plug was non existent They tried to make a replacement but that did not work The bathroom could have done with being refurbished There was no traffic noise as the small window faced the back of the Lycee Francais which was behind the hotel and all one heard was when the pupils were playing football before and after school The lady at reception said as I was in a double room I would have to pay extra I refused The only extra I paid was for Continental and 1 Full English breakfasts which I had had and were excellent with very helpful staff   The Continental or Full English Breakfasts were excellent The staff were v g and helpful

There was nothing I particularly didn t like If i were to be really really picky the room might have been a bit small if I was travelling with my partner but then I am a larger lady so it might not bother others Other than that although the room had two hairdryers I was glad I had brought my own as they weren t really suitable for drying long hair Again not a problem for the majority of the population and not unusual in hotels   The welcome was incredibly friendly The reception staff truly are superb My room was perfect for one person travelling on their own clean and very close to the Natural History Museum and the tube The room had all the necessary facilities including a safe that held a small laptop Highly recommend

My single room was very small but adequate for a one night stay at least it had a good en suite Floor standing air conditioning unit was VERY noisy and could not open window as tube from unit went out through window so window was fixed shut However it was very effective in cooling the room quickly on a very hot London day but you would not want it on all night Did not sleep very well due to poor quality of pillows One was thin and hard the other very lumpy I did bring this to the attention of the receptionist when I checked out and she said she would change the lumpy one Very unusual to see a sign in bathroom saying not to drink tap water and had to pay 2 for a bottle of water If tap water not drinkable I think hotel should provide a bottle of water in room Wallpaper rather faded and could do with updating   Very near South Kensington Tube and hotel just round the corner from Natural History Museum Pleasant reception staff and porter In spite of my niggles it suited me for a one night stay but 109 a bit steep however it is central London and a lot cheaper than places like Travelodge and Premier inns I would think the double rooms would be good A nice feel about the hotel

We were shown 4 rooms They were all tired and shabby and well overpriced The bed was uncomfortable The tiles in the shower were cracked The handle had fallen off the draw and the shower almost scalded us each time we used it The room was not comfortable This was fefinstely not 4 star The bar didn t even have any wine when we wanted a drink in the evening   The lobby was nicely decorated

Our room certainly did not resemble any of the ones pictured It was dark gloomy and was at basement level I booked a family room for myself husband and daughter based upon the photographs on the website I would not choose a dark windowless room for my daughter to sleep in and was relieved we were in and out of there prior to the warmer days as I believe it would have been very unpleasant in the airless rooms The shower nozzle was constantly falling off and hitting us on the head The water temperature constantly changed from hot to cold   The hotel was convenient for our stay in London It was easy to find
Very friendly reception Gave me iced tea Lovely foyer Comfortable room with teaetc and good shower Didn t have time to try the food though

The room we were allocated was in the basement and definitely not the room in the picture when we booked It had only one window which was up fairly high and the school behind played football every night until about 7 30 pm and quite often the ball would hit the window At the very least there should be a security grill or something over the window so that the balls from the school do not hit the window The bathroom left more than a little to be desired as the water from the shower would leak through the gap in the glass partition and end up on the floor where we had to use hand towels to mop it up Also the T V reception was quite poor and we only could get a couple of channels We also asked for room service one night and were told that we would have to go across the road to your sister hotel and get our own room service If we had realized we were going to be in a 2 star accommodation hotel we would have booked elsewhere   The only good things about our stay was the Continental breakfast each morning was really good with a nice variety of cereals and fruit and the flaky croissants were just beautiful The proximity to the restaurants coffee shops and entertainment was the best and was the saving grace of our stay Lara on reception was a god send and helped us every day and Elena the waitress at breakfast was also most helpful and always had a smile on her face

We stopped at this hotel for just one night thankfully When we wanted to give feedback about our experience the available representative was Lara We started discussing the following points with Lara and she fully agreed with us stating I know but I have to work here When we were asked to settle the bill and complain afterwards I declined and wanted to discuss the issues now Well Lara s attitude really turned aggressive rolling eyes and all her conversation was now conducted through shouting When we asked to see the manager Lena we were told she was unavailable as on holiday Lara was able to converse with her during the time we provided the feedback but we were not allowed to talk to her Discussion points in brief The hotel was a 4 star hotel But obviously needs money and a lot of work doing to bring it to even a reasonable level certainly to our room When we checked in we were left waiting whilst Lara talked to people that arrived after us When we were trying to complain about the accommodation we were left 40 minutes whilst the maintenance man verified the state of the room and appliances that did not work The room was grubby bits broken walls and furniture had chunks out of it discoloured and in the bathroom mould due to water damage tv didn t work the kettle worked intermittently shower head didn t fix to bracket so you had to hold whilst showering freezing cold water for the sink During breakfast I asked for tomato ketchup there was none When my wife asked for semi skimmed milk for her cornflakes she was brought hot milk when asked for more cold milk for her cornflakes she was informed there was none left Hearing this Lara brought the waitress out to confirm the story she told the waitress there was milk wasn t there she was twice told they had run out before she ordered her go   The location

Emne Hotel Rating Good morning I visited London for a few days partly private and partly business stay We ended up booking one night through Booking com after looking for 4 stars hotel in the South Kensington area This hotel was the Gainsborough Hotel 7 11 Queensberry Place a hotel rated as a 4 stars hotel by AA I am travelling a lot sometimes over 150 traveling days per year I have never I staying in a 4 star rated hotel with such old and low standard The staff was welcoming and helpful in every way but the standard of the hotel was definitely NOT anything near what one should expect when booking a 4 stars hotel the standard of this hotel is what one could expect if booking a 2 stars hotel The hotel was old and everything had a low and very worn out standard not even a 220v European type socket for shaving machine in the newest and recently renovated bath room No heating air condition except for a small electric oven placed in a corner of the room one could switch on off manually oneself No heating in bathroom too cold to wanting to take a shower The guest areas of the hotel in general were as said old fashioned and very worn out from furniture toilets wallpaper etc etc to standard of dining room kitchen etc We were told we got a renovated room which was correct but the room standard was still extremely low If I was given an older room with an even older and worn out standard would I have not have been stayng there We fail to understand how AA as late as this year could rate this and similar type and standard hotel to more than 2 stars To give this hotel a 4 star rating is the same as misleading guests would almost say cheating guests who believe they have booked a 4 stars almost top class hotel When hotels like Hilton get 5 stars is this hotel no more than a 2 star hotel not even close to 3 stars   Beliggenheten og vennlig ansatte Det er alt positivt som kan sies

Checked in and received a twin room instead of a double room the hotel did not have any double rooms lefts that night and the guy at the counter could only offer us free continental breakfasts the next day to make up for it We already had breakfast plans so no thanks Plus he kept repeating it was also a superior room so it was the same Sorry but its not It s like if I order a steak at a pretty boring Just one type of ham and one type of cheese were offered plus some jams honey jogurt milk and cereals For the full breakfast there s a small menu with choice of eggs beans mushrooms etc but I d rather eat elsewhere There are tons of places nearby Beds are short both rooms and the comforter those standard camel hotel felts was just sandwiched between 2 sheets so the layers separated if you know what I mean The scratchy felt layer inevitably creeps onto your face at night Oh also genius members are supposed to get welcome drinks but that didn t happen   Very good location walkable to museums and subway There s also a lot of cute cafes and restaurants nearby The staffs during the day are super friendly One fixed our room situation the next day and offered free full breakfast for better places can be had for more I think people here give bad ratings because they are used to paying this price for better hotels yea in cheaper cities not London Would definitely stay here again
